What is abnormal to see: Use of nmap, who, w, last, ip a, ifconfig, netstat, ss, arp, cat /etc/passwd, uname -a, lscpu, lsblk in unusual context
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Shows whether someone performed host/system discovery manually

What command to run: for u in /home/*; do cat $u/.bash_history 2>/dev/null; done
What is abnormal to see: Same recon commands across multiple users
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Detects lateral recon or attacker checking accounts

What is abnormal to see: HISTSIZE = 0 (history disabled) unexpectedly
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Indicates possible attempt to avoid leaving recon traces

What is abnormal to see: Logins from unusual IP ranges or at odd hours
Many failed attempts before successful login
What value or knowledge it gives about t: May indicate brute force followed by recon

What is abnormal to see: Login followed by immediate logout and repeated short sessions
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Short sessions often indicate automated recon

What is abnormal to see: Recently accessed unexpectedly or reviewed by unknown user
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Attackers often enumerate users for privilege targeting

What is abnormal to see: Many sudo attempts across users
Interfaces in promiscuous mode (PROMISC) unexpectedly
What value or knowledge it gives about t: May indicate sniffing or recon of network structure

What is abnormal to see: Large number of discovered hosts
Strange routes to unknown subnets
Queries to suspicious domains or internal enumeration
Many internal IPs added in short timeframe
Scripts that list system info unexpectedly
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Some attackers automate recon via scheduled scripts

What is abnormal to see: Presence of scanning tools like nmap, masscan, netcat on non-admin system
Many unusual listening ports
What value or knowledge it gives about t: Shows system surface that attacker may have mapped

What is abnormal to see: Variables altered to gather system info
Recently used in suspicious session
Usage in non-admin environment
Unexpected remote shares mounted
Added internal IP mappings
